volumeTitle: Children of Heracles. Hippolytus. Andromache. Hecuba
label: Children of Heracles (Section 682 - Section 794)

sections.label: Section 682
sections.original: ἥκιστα πρὸς σοῦ μῶρον ἦν εἰπεῖν ἔπος .
sections.translation: It least of all becomes thee thus to utter words of folly .

sections.label: Section 683
sections.original: καὶ μὴ μετασχεῖν γʼ ἀλκίμου μάχης φίλοις .
sections.translation: Far less to shrink from sharing with my friends the stubborn fight .

sections.label: Section 684
sections.original: οὐκ ἔστʼ ἐν ὄψει τραῦμα μὴ δρώσης χερός .
sections.translation: Mere looks can wound no one , if the arm do naught .

sections.label: Section 685
sections.original: τί δʼ ; οὐ σθένοιμι κἂν ἐγὼ δι ’ ἀσπίδος ;
sections.translation: Why , cannot I smite even through their shields ?

sections.label: Section 686
sections.original: σθένοις ἄν , ἀλλὰ πρόσθεν αὐτὸς ἂν πέσοις .
sections.translation: Smite perhaps , more likely be smitten thyself .

sections.label: Section 687
sections.original: οὐδεὶς ἔμʼ ἐχθρῶν προσβλέπων ἀνέξεται .
sections.translation: No foe will dare to meet me face to face .

sections.label: Section 688
sections.original: οὐκ ἔστιν , ὦ τᾶν , ἥ ποτʼ ἦν ῥώμη σέθεν .
sections.translation: Friend , the strength , that erst was thine , is thine no more ,

sections.label: Section 689
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὖν μαχοῦνται γʼ ἀριθμὸν οὐκ ἐλάσσοσι .
sections.translation: Well , at any rate , I will fight with as many as ever I did .

sections.label: Section 690
sections.original: σμικρὸν τὸ σὸν σήκωμα προστίθης φίλοις .
sections.translation: Small the weight thou canst throw into the balance for thy friends .

sections.label: Section 691
sections.original: μή τοί μʼ ἔρυκε δρᾶν παρεσκευασμένον .
sections.translation: Detain me not , when I have girded myself for action .

sections.label: Section 692
sections.original: δρᾶν μὲν σύ γʼ οὐχ οἷός τε , βούλεσθαι δʼ ἴσως .
sections.translation: The power to act is thine no more , the will maybe is there .

sections.label: Section 693
sections.original: ὡς μὴ μενοῦντα τἄλλα σοι λέγειν πάρα .
sections.translation: Stay here I will not , say what else [*] thou wilt

sections.label: Section 694
sections.original: πῶς οὖν ὁπλίταις τευχέων ἄτερ φανῇ ;
sections.translation: How shalt thou show thyself before the troops unarmed ?

sections.label: Section 695
sections.original: ἔστʼ ἐν δόμοισιν ἔνδον αἰχμάλωθʼ ὅπλα ,
sections.translation: There be captured arms within this shrine ; these will I use , and , if I live , restore ; and , if I am slain , the god will not demand them of me back . Go thou within , and from its peg take down a suit of armour and forthwith bring it to me .

sections.label: Section 700
sections.original: αἰσχρὸν γὰρ οἰκούρημα γίγνεται τόδε ,
sections.translation: To linger thus at home is infamous , while some go fight , and others out of cowardice remain behind . [ Exit Servant .

sections.label: Section 702
sections.original: λῆμα μὲν οὔπω στόρνυσι χρόνος
sections.translation: Not yet hath time laid low thy spirit , ’ tis young as ever ; but thy body ’ s strength is gone . Why toil to no purpose ?

sections.label: Section 705
sections.original: σμικρὰ δʼ ὀνήσει πόλιν ἡμετέραν ;
sections.translation: ’ Twill do thee hurt and benefit our city little . At thy age thou shouldst confess thy error and let impossibilities alone . Thou canst in no way get thy vigour back again .

sections.label: Section 709
sections.original: τί χρῆμα μέλλεις σῶν φρενῶν οὐκ ἔνδον ὢν
sections.translation: What means this mad resolve

sections.label: Section 710
sections.original: λιπεῖν μʼ ἔρημον σὺν τέκνοις ἐμοῖς ; ⋯
sections.translation: to leave me with my children undefended here ?

sections.label: Section 711
sections.original: ἀνδρῶν γὰρ ἀλκή · σοὶ δὲ χρῆν τούτων μέλειν .
sections.translation: Men must fight ; and thou must look to them .

sections.label: Section 712
sections.original: τί δʼ ; ἢν θάνῃς σύ , πῶς ἐγὼ σωθήσομαι ;
sections.translation: And what if thou art slain ? what safety shall I find ?

sections.label: Section 713
sections.original: παιδὸς μελήσει παισὶ τοῖς λελειμμένοις .
sections.translation: Thy son ’ s surviving children will care for thee .

sections.label: Section 714
sections.original: ἢν δʼ οὖν , ὃ μὴ γένοιτο , χρήσωνται τύχῃ ;
sections.translation: Suppose they meet with some reverse ? which Heaven forefend !

sections.label: Section 715
sections.original: οἵδ ’ οὐ προδώσουσίν σε , μὴ τρέσῃς , ξένοι .
sections.translation: These strangers will not give thee up , fear not .

sections.label: Section 716
sections.original: τοσόνδε γάρ τοι θάρσος , οὐδὲν ἄλλʼ ἔχω .
sections.translation: They are my last and only hope , I have no other .

sections.label: Section 717
sections.original: καὶ Ζηνὶ τῶν σῶν , οἶδʼ ἐγώ , μέλει πόνων .
sections.translation: Zeus too , I feel sure , cares for thy sufferings .

sections.label: Section 718
sections.original: φεῦ ·
sections.translation: Ah ! of Zeus will I never speak ill , but himself doth know , whether he is just to me .

sections.label: Section 720
sections.original: ὅπλων μὲν ἤδη τήνδʼ ὁρᾷς παντευχίαν ,
sections.translation: Lo ! here thou seest a full coat of mail ; make haste to case thyself therein ; for the strife is nigh , and bitterly doth Ares loathe loiterers ; but if thou fear the weight of the armour , go for the present without it , and in the ranks

sections.label: Section 725
sections.original: κόσμῳ πυκάζου τῷδʼ · ἐγὼ δʼ οἴσω τέως .
sections.translation: do on this gear ; meantime will I carry it .

sections.label: Section 726
sections.original: καλῶς ἔλεξας · ἀλλʼ ἐμοὶ πρόχειρʼ ἔχων
sections.translation: Well said ! keep the harness ready to my hand , put a spear within my grasp , and support me on the left side , guiding my steps .

sections.label: Section 729
sections.original: ἦ παιδαγωγεῖν γὰρ τὸν ὁπλίτην χρεών ;
sections.translation: Am I to lead this warrior like a child ?

sections.label: Section 730
sections.original: ὄρνιθος οὕνεκʼ ἀσφαλῶς πορευτέον .
sections.translation: To save the omen , we must go without stumbling .

sections.label: Section 731
sections.original: εἴθʼ ἦσθα δυνατὸς δρᾶν ὅσον πρόθυμος εἶ .
sections.translation: Would thy power to act were equal to thy zeal !

sections.label: Section 732
sections.original: ἔπειγε · λειφθεὶς δεινὰ πείσομαι μάχης .
sections.translation: Hasten ; I shall feel it grievously , if I am too late for the battle .

sections.label: Section 733
sections.original: σύ τοι βραδύνεις , οὐκ ἐγὼ δοκῶ τι δρᾶν .
sections.translation: ’ Tis thou who art slow , not I , though thou fanciest thou art doing wonders .

sections.label: Section 734
sections.original: οὔκουν ὁρᾷς μου κῶλον ὡς ἐπείγεται ;
sections.translation: Dost not mark how swift my steps are hasting ?

sections.label: Section 735
sections.original: ὁρῶ δοκοῦντα μᾶλλον ἢ σπεύδοντά σε .
sections.translation: I mark more seeming than reality in thy haste .

sections.label: Section 736
sections.original: οὐ ταῦτα λέξεις , ἡνίκʼ ἂν λεύσσῃς μʼ ἐκεῖ .
sections.translation: Thou wilt tell a different tale when thou seest me there .

sections.label: Section 737
sections.original: τί δρῶντα ; βουλοίμην δʼ ἂν εὐτυχοῦντά γε .
sections.translation: What shall I see thee do ? I wish thee all success , at any rate .

sections.label: Section 738
sections.original: διʼ ἀσπίδος θείνοντα πολεμίων τινά .
sections.translation: Thou shalt see me smite some foeman through the shield .

sections.label: Section 739
sections.original: εἰ δή ποθʼ ἥξομέν γε · τοῦτο γὰρ φόβος .
sections.translation: Perhaps , if ever we get there . I have my fears of that .

sections.label: Section 740
sections.original: φεῦ ·
sections.translation: Ah ! would to Heaven that thou , mine arm , e ’ en as I remember thee in thy lusty youth , when with Heracles thou didst sack Sparta , couldst so champion me to-day ! how I would put Eurystheus to flight ! since he is too craven to wait the onslaught .

sections.label: Section 745
sections.original: ἔστιν δʼ ἐν ὄλβῳ καὶ τόδʼ οὐκ ὀρθῶς ἔχον ,
sections.translation: For prosperity carries with it this error too a reputation for bravery ; for we think the prosperous man a master of all knowledge .

sections.label: Section 1.748
sections.original: Γᾶ καὶ παννύχιος σελά-
sections.translation: O earth , and moon that shines by night , and dazzling radiance of the god ,

sections.label: Section 1.750
sections.original: φαεσίμβροτοι αὐγαί ,
sections.translation: that giveth light to man , bear the tidings to me , shout aloud to heaven for joy , and beside our ruler ’ s throne , and in the shrine of grey-eyed Athene .

sections.label: Section 1.755
sections.original: μέλλω τᾶς πατριώτιδος
sections.translation: For my fatherland and home will I soon decide the issue of the strife with the gleaming sword , because I have taken suppliants under my protection .

sections.label: Section 1.759
sections.original: δεινὸν μὲν πόλιν ὡς Μυκή-
sections.translation: ’ Tis a fearful thing , that a city

sections.label: Section 1.760
sections.original: νας εὐδαίμονα καὶ δορὸς
sections.translation: prosperous as Mycenae is , one famed for martial prowess , should harbour wrath against my land ; still , my countrymen , it were a shameful thing in us to yield up suppliant strangers

sections.label: Section 1.765
sections.original: κελεύμασιν Ἄργους .
sections.translation: at the bidding of Argos . Zeus is on my side , I am not afraid ; Zeus hath a favour unto me , as is my due ; never by me [*] shall gods be thought weaker than mortal men .

sections.label: Section 2.770
sections.original: ἀλλʼ , ὦ πότνια — σὸν γὰρ οὖ-
sections.translation: O dread goddess , [*] thine the soil whereon we stand , thine this city , for thou art its mother , queen , and saviour ; wherefore turn some other way the impious king , who leadeth a host

sections.label: Section 2.775
sections.original: στρατὸν Ἀργόθεν · οὐ γὰρ ἐμᾷ γʼ ἀρετᾷ
sections.translation: from Argos with brandished lance against this land ; for , such my worth , I little merit exile from my home .

sections.label: Section 2.777
sections.original: ἐπεί σοι πολύθυτος ἀεὶ
sections.translation: For thy worship [*] is aye performed with many a sacrifice , and never art thou forgotten as each month draweth to its close ,

sections.label: Section 2.780
sections.original: νέων τʼ ἀοιδαὶ χορῶν τε μολπαί .
sections.translation: when young voices sing and dancers ’ music is heard abroad , while on our wind-swept hill goes up the cry of joy to the beat of maidens ’ feet by night .

sections.label: Section 784
sections.original: δέσποινα , μύθους σοί τε συντομωτάτους
sections.translation: Mistress , the message that I bring is very short

sections.label: Section 785
sections.original: κλύειν ἐμοί τε τῷδε καλλίστους φέρω .
sections.translation: for thee to hear and fair for me , who stand before thee , to announce . O ’ er our foes we are victorious , and trophies are being set up , with panoplies upon them , taken from thy enemies .

sections.label: Section 788
sections.original: ὦ φίλταθʼ , ἥδε σʼ ἡμέρα διήλασεν
sections.translation: Best of friends ! this day hath wrought [*] thy liberty by reason of these tidings .

sections.label: Section 790
sections.original: μιᾶς δʼ ἔμʼ οὔπω συμφορᾶς ἐλευθεροῖς ·
sections.translation: But there still remains one anxious thought thou dost not free me from ;— a thought of fear ;— are those , whose lives I cherish , spared to me ?

sections.label: Section 792
sections.original: ζῶσιν μέγιστόν γʼ εὐκλεεῖς κατὰ στρατόν .
sections.translation: They are , and high their fame through all the army spreads .

sections.label: Section 793
sections.original: ὁ μὲν γέρων οὐκ ἔστιν Ἰόλεως ὅδε ;
sections.translation: The old man Iolaus ,— is he yet alive ?

sections.label: Section 794
sections.original: μάλιστα , πράξας δʼ ἐκ θεῶν κάλλιστα δή .
sections.translation: Aye , that he is , a hero whom the gods delight to honour .
